section.name: invariants
section.what: Agency-preserving philosophy — the rules of the system

section.name: skill-catalog
section.what: All 27 skills with levels, triggers, and costs

section.name: agent-catalog
section.what: All 14 agents with capabilities, tools, and model requirements

section.name: mcp-servers
section.what: 2 optional Rust MCP servers (GitHub, Sequential Thinking)

section.name: progression
section.what: L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 capability model

section.name: references
section.what: Papers and prior art that inform the design

emergency-start.step: Start a Claude Code session — skills auto-inject via hooks
Try: "Use the brainstorming skill to design a feature"

invariants.invariant: Skills recommend, they don't command. Every strong recommendation has a documented skip cost. Every prohibition has a documented exception. The agent retains judgment.
Run the verification command. Read the output. Then make the claim. Never say "should work" or "looks correct" without evidence.
Search for existing solutions before proposing new ones. Extension is cheaper than duplication. Every parallel system is debt.
Workers with multiple tasks produce partial completions. Split into focused units. Each worker finishes within the tool-count budget.
Workers don't coordinate mid-task. Everything they need goes into the initial prompt. If a worker needs to ask a question, you failed at decomposition.
Compulsion language (MUST without escape, NEVER without exception, NOT NEGOTIABLE) is detected by scanner.py and flagged. Skills use "strongly recommended" with skip costs.

skill.name: using-kinderpowers
skill.description: Establishes how skills work as invitations with documented costs, not commands without escape

skill.name: brainstorming
skill.trigger: before creative work, features, components, behavior changes
skill.description: Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation

skill.name: test-driven-development
skill.description: Write failing test → implement → verify green → commit

skill.name: systematic-debugging
skill.trigger: any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior
skill.description: Structured hypothesis tracking and elimination

skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.trigger: about to claim work is complete
skill.description: Evidence before assertions — run the command, read the output, then claim

skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Structured review request with context for reviewer

skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Technical rigor in response — verify suggestions before implementing

skill.name: writing-plans
skill.trigger: spec or requirements for multi-step task
skill.description: Comprehensive implementation plans with bite-sized tasks, discovery-before-creation

skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Batch execution with checkpoints, claim protocol, verify-before-assuming

skill.name: strategic-planning
skill.trigger: work needs design or discovery before execution
skill.description: Strategic briefs with investigative and implementation modes

skill.name: metathinking
skill.trigger: complex decisions, debugging mysteries, architectural choices
skill.description: Sequential thinking with mandatory branching and confidence tracking

skill.name: research-extraction
skill.trigger: analyzing external codebases, libraries, or papers
skill.description: Harvest → extract → analyze → rank → verify pipeline

skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Fresh subagent per task with review between tasks

skill.name: using-git-worktrees
skill.description: Isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection

skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Guides merge, PR, or cleanup decisions

skill.name: remembering-conversations
skill.description: Searches conversation history for relevant context

skill.name: find-skills
skill.description: Discovers and installs agent skills

skill.name: writing-skills
skill.description: Skill authoring with scanner compliance

skill.name: beads
skill.description: Issue tracking with persistent memory across sessions

skill.name: requirements
skill.description: Structured requirements elicitation

skill.name: retrospective
skill.trigger: after completing a project or phase
skill.description: Structured reflection on what worked and what didn't

skill.name: architecture
skill.description: Architecture decision records and trade-off analysis

skill.name: adversarial-review
skill.description: Adversarial quality review looking for what's missing

skill.name: team-orchestration
skill.description: Team creation, task assignment, worker sizing, INJECT patterns

skill.name: comprehensive-pr-review
skill.trigger: reviewing PRs, team-based code review, PR quality gates
skill.description: 7-persona team review with tiered depth, pedanticness slider, debate, and self-improvement loop

skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Parallel dispatch without shared state

skill.name: dispatching-to-runtimes
skill.description: Prompt structuring for Gemini, GPT, and local models

agent.name: code-reviewer
agent.description: Post-implementation review against plan and coding standards.
agent.when: After completing a logical chunk of work — feature, bugfix, or refactor step.
agent.output: Structured review with findings, severity, and specific file:line references.

agent.name: multi-perspective-review
agent.description: Council-mode review: spawns 2-5 disposable lens agents, each reviewing the same artifact from a different perspective. Smart persona selection based on what could break. Default: 5 lenses. Tunable via prompt. Inspired by council-mode patterns.
agent.when: Significant work needs review from multiple angles — API design, security changes, architecture.
agent.lenses: Edge Case, Contract, Empathy, Resilience, Performance, Security, Accessibility
agent.output: Per-lens findings merged into a single prioritized review with cross-lens conflicts noted.

see-also.note: Third Alternative pattern applies to lens selection

agent.name: strategic-planner
agent.description: Goal → discovery → approach selection → phased plan with dependencies. Investigative mode for unknowns, implementation mode for clear specs.
agent.when: Vague feature requests, complex refactors, anything needing scoping before execution.
agent.output: Strategic brief with phases, dependencies, risk assessment, and success criteria.

agent.name: quality-gate
agent.description: Adversarial verification — refuses to pass without evidence. Runs checks, compares against requirements, looks for what was missed.
agent.when: Work claimed as complete. Feature ready for merge. Phase boundary.
agent.output: PASS/FAIL verdict with evidence chain. Failing items include specific remediation.

agent.name: team-coordinator
agent.description: Orchestrates parallel Claude Code agent teams. Decomposes work into non-overlapping file domains, spawns workers, manages task assignment and integration.
agent.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Agent, Write, Edit
agent.when: Large tasks that benefit from parallelization — multi-module refactors, feature rollouts.
agent.constraints: Non-overlapping file domains per worker. One task per worker. INJECT upfront.
agent.output: Coordinated multi-agent execution with integrated results.

agent.name: research-extractor
agent.description: Three modes: idea extraction (harvest→extract→rank→verify), usage evaluation (assess capabilities/limitations/fit), deep integration (API surface analysis, migration plan).
agent.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, WebSearch, WebFetch
agent.when: Evaluating libraries, analyzing reference implementations, extracting ideas from papers.
agent.output: Ranked findings with implementation sketches, difficulty ratings, and source links.

see-also.note: harvest → extract → analyze → rank → verify pipeline

agent.name: gsd-codebase-mapper
agent.description: Explores codebase with parallel focus areas (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes structured analysis documents to reduce orchestrator context load.

agent.name: gsd-debugger
agent.description: Scientific method debugging: hypothesis → test → narrow → fix. Manages debug sessions with checkpoints for crash recovery. Parameters control assertion depth.
agent.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch

agent.name: gsd-executor
agent.description: Executes plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator. Parameters control rollback strictness.
agent.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob

agent.name: gsd-researcher
agent.description: Parameterized research agent with 3 modes: phase (implementation research), project (domain ecosystem), synthesize (merge outputs). Replaces gsd-phase-researcher, gsd-project-researcher, gsd-research-synthesizer.
agent.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, SendMessage

agent.name: gsd-verifier
agent.description: Parameterized verification agent with 4 modes: goal-backward (phase goals), integration (cross-phase E2E), plan-quality (pre-execution check), coverage (test gap filling). Replaces gsd-verifier, gsd-integration-checker, gsd-plan-checker, gsd-nyquist-auditor.
agent.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, SendMessage

agent.name: gsd-ui
agent.description: Parameterized UI agent with 3 modes: spec (design contract), audit (6-pillar visual review), validate (spec compliance check). Replaces gsd-ui-researcher, gsd-ui-auditor, gsd-ui-checker.
agent.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, SendMessage

agent.name: gsd-planner
agent.description: Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. scope=project absorbs roadmapper functionality.
agent.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, SendMessage

agent.name: gsd-user-profiler
agent.description: Analyzes session messages across 8 behavioral dimensions to produce a scored developer profile with confidence levels and evidence.

command.name: /gsd:plan-phase
command.when: Ready to plan a specific phase

command.name: /gsd:progress
command.description: Show context and route to next action

command.name: /gsd:ship
command.description: PR creation with review and merge prep

rule.name: verification-required
rule.description: Blocks completion without verification evidence in transcript

rule.name: discovery-before-creation
rule.description: Warns before creating files without evidence of search

rule.name: brainstorm-before-build
rule.description: Warns before writing 100+ lines without design discussion

level.name: Coding Assistant
level.description: Agent writes code, tests it, asks for review

level.name: Agentic Worker
level.description: Agent plans work, tracks progress, works in isolation
level.capability: Plans, memory, worktrees, strategic-planning, metathinking, research-extraction

level.name: Team Orchestrator
level.capability: Team orchestration, parallel dispatch, runtime dispatch, quality gates

level.name: Dark Factory
level.capability: GSD lifecycle engine, hookify enforcement, autonomous execution

server.name: kp-github-mcp
server.description: Token-compressed GitHub MCP server. 10-40x fewer tokens per read vs official plugin.
server.tools: Issues, PRs, commits, branches, releases, tags, files, repos, actions, labels, teams, users, code search.

features.feature: 5-stage compression pipeline: strip waste → flatten → project → compact → auto-format
Field projection (fields param) and output format control (json/table/text)
reqwest HTTP primary (0.5s), gh CLI fallback

server.name: kp-sequential-thinking
server.description: Enhanced sequential thinking with hints (not mandates), branching, merge, confidence tracking, Dunning-Kruger detection, and per-model tuning profiles.
server.tools: sequentialthinking — the caller controls depth, branching, and when to act on hints.

features.feature: Hints system: non-prescriptive observations (linear_chain, premature_confidence, merge_available, etc.)
Branch merge: continuation_mode="merge" with merge_branches to synthesize insights
Dunning-Kruger detection: flags confidence > 80% at layer 1 as "observation"
Per-model profiles: Claude, Gemini Flash, DeepSeek, Grok, Llama/Nemotron, custom via JSON
JSONL logging for learning pipeline harvesting
Brenner "Third Alternative" and Four Self-Checks in first-call guidance

recommended.note: Not dependencies — kinderpowers works without these. But they amplify it.

tool.name: beads
tool.description: Dolt-powered issue tracker for multi-session work. Persistent memory across conversation compaction. Dependencies, blockers, priorities. The kinderpowers beads skill wraps this CLI.
tool.why: Without beads, work context is lost when conversations compact or sessions end. With beads, every agent picks up where the last one left off.

tool.name: beads-viewer
tool.description: Web UI for beads issues. Dependency graphs, timeline views, status dashboards. Read-only — safe to run alongside agents.
tool.why: Agents work in CLI. Humans need visual context. beads-viewer bridges the gap.

tool.name: ck
tool.description: Local-first semantic and hybrid BM25/grep search tool. Rust, fast, works offline. Indexes codebases for semantic search without cloud dependencies.
tool.why: The codebase mapper and research agents need search. ck provides it locally and openly. No API keys, no rate limits, no privacy concerns.
tool.integration: gsd-codebase-mapper can emit JSONL that ck indexes. Research agents can query ck for semantic code search during planning and debugging.

tool.name: ultimate-bug-scanner
tool.description: Multi-strategy bug scanner: static analysis, pattern matching, LLM-assisted review. Finds bugs that linters miss.
tool.why: The quality-gate agent and verification-before-completion skill verify work is correct. ultimate-bug-scanner finds the bugs they'd miss — different detection strategies are complementary, not redundant.

tool.name: ucp
tool.description: Open standard for commerce interoperability between AI agents, apps, businesses, and payment providers. Capability discovery, checkout sessions, personalized commerce.
tool.why: Agents doing research, planning, or execution may need to discover and interact with commerce services. UCP provides the standard protocol — agents don't need custom integrations per vendor.
tool.integration: GSD agents can discover business capabilities via UCP during research phases. The comprehensive-pr-review skill can check UCP compliance in commerce-related PRs.

tool.name: whiteout
tool.description: PII scrubbing for LLM sessions. Tokenizes sensitive data before it reaches upstream providers, de-tokenizes on return. CLI for scrubbing local session logs.
tool.why: Agents working with personal data (legal, medical, financial) need PII protection. whiteout scan wraps cleanly as a pre-session check. Complements veil scanner (secrets) with PII-layer coverage.

tool.name: headless-pm
tool.description: REST API + MCP server for coordinating multiple LLM agents across a shared task hierarchy (epic to feature to task) with Git workflow integration.
tool.why: Alternative to beads for L3 team coordination. Persistent task store with API access. Agents working in separate directories can share a task board without git-based beads.

paper.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
paper.authors: Yao, Yu, Zhao, Shafran, Griffiths, Cao, Narasimhan
paper.informs: Branching architecture in kp-sequential-thinking. ToT frames reasoning as tree search with branching and evaluation — our branchFromThought + branchId implements this.

paper.title: Graph of Thoughts: Solving Elaborate Problems with Large Language Models
paper.authors: Besta, Blach, Kubicek, Gerstenberger, Podstawski, Gianinazzi, Gajda, Lehmann, Nyczyk, Niewiadomski, Hoefler
paper.informs: Branch merge capability. GoT extends ToT with arbitrary graph structure where thoughts can merge — our continuation_mode=merge with merge_branches implements convergence. GoT showed 62% quality improvement over ToT on sorting with 31% cost reduction.

paper.title: Adaptive Graph of Thoughts: Test-Time Adaptive Reasoning
paper.informs: Unified framework insight. AGoT showed +46.2% on GPQA by dynamically choosing between linear (CoT), branching (ToT), and graph (GoT) strategies. Our hints system lets the caller make this choice rather than the server enforcing it.

paper.title: Deep Think with Confidence
paper.informs: Confidence-based trace pruning. DeepConf filters low-confidence reasoning traces and terminates early when confidence drops. Our low_confidence_pattern hint surfaces this signal.

paper.title: Cycles of Thought: Measuring LLM Confidence through Stable Explanations
paper.informs: Stability-based confidence. Measures confidence by checking explanation stability across rephrased queries. Our layer_available hint encourages calibrating confidence against reasoning depth.

paper.title: Fact-and-Reflection Improves Confidence Calibration
paper.informs: Two-pass calibration pattern. FaR generates facts first, then reflects to calibrate. Maps to our layer progression: layer 1 = fact gathering, layer 2 = reflection. Enforcing layer 2+ for high confidence is informed by this.

paper.title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect in Large Language Models
paper.informs: Premature confidence detection. LLMs show systematic overconfidence on tasks they handle poorly — mirroring the Dunning-Kruger effect. Our premature_confidence hint flags high confidence at layer 1 as an observation for the caller.

citation.author: Sydney Brenner
citation.text: Someone said, 'Either model A is right or model B is right.' And I said, 'You've forgotten there's a third alternative... Both could be wrong.'
citation.informs: Third Alternative pattern in metathinking skill and first-call guidance. Prevents false dichotomy traps in reasoning.

project.author: Jesse Vincent
project.informs: Craft philosophy, skill format, scanner, hook system. The foundation layer.

project.author: Davíd Braun
project.informs: Lifecycle engine, 42 commands, 16 agents, workflow machinery. The execution layer.

project.author: Diego Perez
project.informs: Enforcement rule format and Claude Code hook framework.
TOON output format specification. Tables over prose, key:value for config.

claim.method: Check name + description in each SKILL.md
